quote_of_day.text: I still don't understand why Animal was hated so much. I enjoyed it more than Dhurandhar, Animal had more literally me moments with that swastika scene. Animal feels more timeless, and a very very very good story on toxic father etc situations. It was a fantastic movie
quote_of_day.name: Anon

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A toxic high pressure system of racist and derogatory comments dominated the board, particularly in the Leading section.

A post describing Sub-Saharan Africans as 'unstable' and 'subhumanoid' sparked a noxious cloud of replies, drawing squalls of agreement and criticism.

A front of political frustration moved in, as one anon criticized Indian leadership, sparking debate and a flurry of responses. This thread brought a brief but intense heat wave of argumentative posts.

Meanwhile, a fog of speculation rolled in around the sudden departure of 'Bharatchan', with one anon asking for information and sparking a small drizzle of replies.

The discussion remained relatively contained, with a few scattered showers of opinion.

articles.title: Anon Poster Describes Sub-Saharan Africans as 'Unstable' and 'Subhumanoid'
articles.body:
An anonymous poster on an Indian imageboard expressed fear that if Sub-Saharan Africans 'flood' a country, its people would be overwhelmed. The poster stated, 'Chaddi mein coom nikal gaya mera darr ke maare. They are so unhinged, man.

Imagine if they flood your country. They have 0 impulse control like a homo erectus.' Another anon responded by referencing a documentary about the Central African Republic war and claimed that leaders from Sub-Saharan Africa are often incompetent and corrupt.

This poster stated that these leaders are not significantly different from the general population. A different poster fantasized about a scenario involving Candace Owens and described a graphic and disturbing scenario.

One respondent brought up the topic of Israel and its military actions, stating that Israel has 'raped nations for less' and highlighting the Entebbe raid. This poster also praised Israel's military capabilities.

Another poster claimed that Sub-Saharan Africans are not even human beings, citing a supposed fact that one-third of their genome is 'non-sapien.' This poster also expressed a desire to engage in a graphic and disturbing act with Candace Owens.

A respondent argued that India is objectively better than Sub-Saharan Africa, citing its economic and technological advancements. This poster also criticized 'blackpillers' for being overly pessimistic.

One poster described defending Sub-Saharan Africans as 'genuinely some of the lowest of low caste subhuman roach hill to die on.' This poster also claimed that Sub-Saharan Africans are not even people and have 'non-human' DNA.

A different respondent discussed the topic of intelligence and claimed that even among 'chimps,' there are those that are exceptionally retarded and those that are above the rest.

This poster questioned why someone would choose to associate with the most retarded individuals. Another poster expressed a desire to keep a 'personal ebony servant' for sexual purposes and discussed the importance of maintaining racial purity.

A respondent argued that Sub-Saharan Africans are in a state of 'primal monkey' state and that they regressed after decolonization.

articles.title: Anon Criticizes Indian Leadership, Sparks Debate
articles.body:
Anon expressed frustration with Indian leaders, stating they spend too much energy on "bhangi reservation scams, caste votebank warfare and 'hisab chukta' politics" instead of focusing on building factories, manufacturing, and R&D.

This, according to Anon, is why India remains a "laughingstock" still importing everything.

Another Anon responded, discussing the potential consequences of abolishing certain practices, suggesting that ethnic groups exceling in various fields might abandon the nation if constantly penalized for their success.

Anon also questioned the feasibility of Indian leaders changing their approach, stating that demography is their destiny and they have no incentive to choose otherwise.

One respondent emphasized the need for India to work on its sewage management and rivers before considering military expansion.

This Anon expressed concern about the country's direction, calling it a "dalit population infested urban heat trap." Other posts touched on the Indian military's capabilities, with some arguing that modernization and expansion are necessary for sustained offensive operations.

The conversation also turned to the topic of infiltration and foreign influence, with one Anon suggesting that the Indian government might be backed by the CIA to keep the country unstable.

The thread included various perspectives on India's development, military capabilities, and leadership, reflecting a range of concerns and criticisms.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on the Sudden Departure of 'Bharatchan'
articles.body:
An anonymous poster (Anon) initiated a discussion on the sudden departure of 'Bharatchan', an individual associated with the imageboard. The OP expressed confusion about the situation, mentioning 'gayush dalit' and inquiring about the board's ownership.

Anon provided some background information, stating that the previous administrator, 'Bharatchan', had been dealing with various issues, including constant reports, raids from other communities, and internal conflicts.

According to this anon, 'Bharatchan' had been subtly hinting at his struggles for months before eventually quitting due to exhaustion.

The conversation took a different turn when another anon mentioned 'motivanon', an individual who had allegedly been doxxed by users on the board. This anon claimed that 'motivanon' had been threatened with doxxing to his Japanese workplace and harassment.

A respondent expressed some regret over the doxxing incident, apologizing to 'motivanon' if he were reading. Anon also brought up 'swissfag shiller vegcuckery', another individual who had apparently been involved in similar activities.

According to this anon, 'swissfag' was a Muslim trying to incite civil war in India and was not even an Indian Muslim.

The discussion also touched on caste-related conflicts and the challenges of managing an Indian imageboard, with anons mentioning issues with reports, raids from other communities, and internal conflicts. One anon described the experience of running an Indian chan as 'literal hell'.

The thread included various claims and counter-claims about individuals and their actions on the board. Anon joked about certain groups and individuals, using derogatory language. A respondent expressed a desire for 'cuck poster' to be doxxed instead.

articles.title: Indians on Pakistan's Existence and Nationalism
articles.body:
Anon posted that if Pakistan vanished, Indian patriotic zeal would plummet. Anon responded, saying Indians don't obsess over Pakistan and have better things to think about.

Another anon argued that Pakistan's existence isn't necessary for Indian nationalism, and India can focus on its economy without defence spending.

Anon claimed low castes should be kept away from the army and bureaucracy, and that India needs anti-reservation policies. A respondent stated Pakistan's survival acts as a glue for Indian identity.

Anon also mentioned that Pakistan used to be ahead of India but is currently in a bad situation. A later post read that even if Pakistan vanished, other threats like China and Bangladesh remain.

articles.title: Anon Praises Bollywood Film 'Animal'
articles.body:
Anon expressed admiration for the Bollywood film 'Animal', calling it a 'fantastic movie' with a 'very good story on toxic father etc situations.' They enjoyed it more than another film, 'Dhurandhar', citing its relatability and timelessness.

Another anon responded, criticizing those who dislike popular films, labeling them 'midwits' trying to appear intelligent. They acknowledged some issues with the film's execution but found it enjoyable overall.

The discussion took a darker turn as an anon mentioned sorting out 'low-caste' individuals they perceived as prone to criminal behavior, sparking a response from another user urging restraint and specificity in such discussions.

Anon also dissected the film's plot and characters, with one user praising its entertainment value and another lamenting its perceived shortcomings, such as poor character development.
